─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Vaultwarden Env Vars ]─ ┐

Vaultwarden Env Vars

Purpose

Load secret notes and custom fields from Vaultwarden into shell environment variables securely.

When to Use

  • You need secrets for API calls (Cloudflare, OPNsense, etc.).
  • You want to avoid printing secrets to stdout or committing them to files.

Process

  1. Unlock: Ensure BW_SESSION is unlocked in the current shell.
  2. Load Note: 
    source scripts/load-vaultwarden-note.sh "Cloudflare API Token" CF_API_TOKEN
  3. Load Custom Field: 
    source scripts/load-vaultwarden-note.sh "OPNsense API Key" OPNSENSE_API_KEY key
  4. Verify: 
    test -n "${CF_API_TOKEN-}" && echo "Variable loaded"

Safety Rules

  • NEVER print secrets to stdout.
  • Prefer ephemeral variables for one-off commands.
  • Keep BW_SESSION in-memory only.

Reference Paths

  • Script: scripts/load-vaultwarden-note.sh
  • Env: .env (gitignored)
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
